LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN BREKKE, CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC'S MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN EVIDENCE FILED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PARTIAL SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST.PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH SPECIFIES THE DOCUMENTS TO BE SEALED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Certain Evidence Filed In Support Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Katherine Alphonso, Esq. 415-926-7600 of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN BREKKE, CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC's Motion To Seal Certain Evidence Filed In Support Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication.

Granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which specifies the documents to be sealed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Apr-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENNIFER ORAZIO'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Jennifer Orazio's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted on the following conditions: (1) Plaintiff shall make herself available for a reopened deposition as to the newly asserted cause of action for retaliatory discharge; (2) the discovery cut-off date is extended by 60 days to July 19; (3) Defendants shall have until August 23 to file a motion for summary adjudication as to the new cause of action, and that motion may be heard on less than 75 days' notice; and (4) the current June 24, 2019 trial date is vacated, and trial is continued to a mutually agreeable date. Counsel shall meet and confer to agree upon a new trial date.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Jennifer Orazio's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted (except for the NIED claim against Cambria) and the trial is continued to June 24, 2019 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date. Ms. Orazio has until October 22, 2018 to file her first amended complaint. Granting leave to amend comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion is eliminated by the continuance of the trial. The court does not address defendants' arguments that the new claims are time-barred or otherwise deficient. Those challenges are best dealt with by a pleading motion such as a demurrer. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk
